It belongs to the order Sapindales, a large order of plants that includes maples, horse chestnuts, and other related species. intro: Pennantia baylisiana, also known as the Kawakawa or New Zealand pepper tree, is a small, deciduous tree native to New Zealand with heart-shaped leaves that resemble those of the pepper tree. The leaves of the Pennantia baylisiana have been traditionally used in Maori medicine for their potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of the plant include alkaloids, particularly kawain and demethylsubesterine, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Maori medicine, the leaves are typically made into a tea or infused in oil to create a topical preparation, and more recently, the plant has been incorporated into various herbal supplements and topical creams.
